name: apple-messages-blade-mcp
title: Apple Messages
description: Apple Messages (iMessage) operations — conversations, search, contacts, attachments, send
tagline: Read, search, and send via iMessage on macOS
author: groupthink-dev
notes: macOS only. Requires Full Disk Access for reading message history. TCC Automation permission for sending.

env.name: MESSAGES_WRITE_ENABLED
env.description: Enable send operations (sending messages is irreversible)

tools.name: messages_info
tools.description: System info: macOS version, FDA status, write gate state, Messages.app status, chat.db path + counts.

tools.name: messages_chats
tools.description: List all conversations with server-side limit. Direct SQL against chat.db.

tools.name: messages_chat
tools.description: Get a specific conversation by chat_id or handle. Server-side WHERE in SQL.

tools.name: messages_messages
tools.description: Get messages from a conversation with server-side chat_id + limit + before-cursor pagination.

tools.name: messages_recent
tools.description: Recent messages across all conversations with server-side limit + since-ISO filter pushed into SQL.

tools.name: messages_search
tools.description: Full-text search across all messages with server-side query + limit (SQL LIKE against text column).

tools.name: messages_contacts
tools.description: List known handles (phone numbers, emails, iCloud accounts) with server-side limit.

tools.name: messages_contact
tools.description: Get details for a specific handle including message count + last interaction.

tools.name: messages_attachments
tools.description: List attachments from a conversation with server-side chat_id + limit (metadata only; never reads file content).

tools.name: messages_attachment_info
tools.description: Get detailed metadata for a specific attachment by attachment_id (never reads file content).

tools.name: messages_stats
tools.description: Aggregate statistics: total chats, messages, date range, top contacts.

tools.name: messages_send
tools.description: Send a text message via AppleScript bridge. Write — gated by MESSAGES_WRITE_ENABLED + confirm; messages cannot be unsent (DD-249 write amendment).

tools.name: messages_send_file
tools.description: Send a file attachment via AppleScript bridge. Write — gated by MESSAGES_WRITE_ENABLED + confirm; files cannot be unsent (DD-249 write amendment).

tools.name: messages_start_group
tools.description: Start a new group conversation via AppleScript bridge. Write — gated by MESSAGES_WRITE_ENABLED + confirm; group creation cannot be undone (DD-249 write amendment).

setup.blurb: Read iMessage and SMS conversations, search message history, and send messages via Apple Messages on macOS.
setup.help_text: Grant Full Disk Access to your terminal emulator (Terminal, iTerm2, Ghostty) in System Settings > Privacy & Security > Full Disk Access. Child processes inherit the grant, so the MCP binary itself does not need separate access.

fields.label: Enable Send Operations
fields.help: Set to 'true' to enable sending messages. All sends require additional confirmation.

prerequisites: Full Disk Access granted to terminal emulator
Messages app configured with iMessage or SMS Relay

highlights.text: Conversations — browse recent chats with contact resolution
Search — full-text search across message history
Attachments — list and locate shared files and images
Send — compose and send iMessages with confirmation gate
Privacy — reads local chat.db, no cloud API or sync

readme:
### What it does

Apple Messages MCP gives your AI agent read access to your iMessage and SMS conversations on macOS. It reads directly from the local Messages database (`chat.db`) — no cloud API, no Apple ID authentication, no network requests for reads.

Your agent can browse recent conversations, search message history by keyword or contact, list attachments, and — when enabled — send messages via AppleScript automation.

### Privacy model

All data stays local. The MCP reads `~/Library/Messages/chat.db` using SQLite, which requires Full Disk Access for the host terminal process. No data leaves the machine for read operations. Contact names are resolved locally from the address book.

### Safety model

Sending messages is irreversible and disabled by default:

- **Read-only by default** — conversations, search, and attachments work immediately
- **Write gate** — set `MESSAGES_WRITE_ENABLED=true` to unlock send operations
- **Confirm gate** — each send requires `confirm: true` per call
- **TCC automation** — macOS will prompt for Automation permission on first send

### Requirements

macOS only. Requires Full Disk Access granted to the terminal emulator (Terminal, iTerm2, Ghostty, or Warp). Child processes inherit the grant, so the MCP binary does not need separate access. Messages app must be configured with iMessage or SMS Relay.

scenarios.label: Search for a past conversation
scenarios.body: Search message history for a keyword or contact, review the matching conversations with timestamps, and extract the relevant details or decisions from the thread.

scenarios.label: Catch up on recent messages
scenarios.body: Browse the most recent conversations, summarise unread threads, and identify any messages that need a reply or follow-up action.
